title: Business Analyst - Digitales Marketing (m/w/d)
required_skills: Performance data / E-Commerce / Commercial Analytics experience
Degree in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Data Science, Digital Business, Mathematik, E-Commerce, Wirtschaftspsychologie or comparable
bonus_skills: Understanding of digital architectures and software development
matched_skills: Customer journey analysis (Hapag-Lloyd funnel & UX work)
Relevant academic degree (Master of Management & Entrepreneurship)
missing_skills: Personalisation mechanics / multivariate test design at scale
core_responsibilities: Analyse transaction, response and interaction data to derive deep customer behaviour and offer-effectiveness insights
Design, execute and evaluate A/B and multivariate tests to measurably improve personalisation, CRM and offer communication
Continuously develop digital customer interactions (EDEKA App) and loyalty programmes
Identify optimisation and innovation potentials across the full customer journey (digital and analogue)
End-to-end ownership from analysis through concept to implementation; translate complex findings into clear decision bases for operational and strategic stakeholders
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Power BI certification (PL-300) and hands-on dashboard/reporting work prominently in the skills section
Lead with A/B testing & experiment design experience — align with JD language around 'Hypothesen testen'
Emphasise e-commerce funnel analytics and audience/behavioural data work from Moli Media
Frame Hapag-Lloyd funnel & UX analysis as customer journey and app-adjacent analytics experience
Showcase C-level data storytelling (HSBC USD 8M programme) to address the stakeholder communication requirement
Mention Jira and Confluence usage in agile delivery context (Hapag-Lloyd sprint work)
If any SQL or Python exposure exists (even basic/coursework), surface it explicitly — these are hard-required
Use 'Ownership', 'data-driven', and 'cross-functional' in the professional summary to match culture keywords

company_info.size_raw: rund 413.000 Mitarbeiter:innen (2024); rund 10.900 Märkte; Umsatz 75,3 Mrd. Euro
